entries.title: Pinned OpenAI-style tool contract
entries.summary: A small deployable assistant contract pins prompt segments, a JSON Schema response envelope, and OpenAI tool definitions, then verifies the repository skeleton and lockfile baseline used by CI.
entries.real_world: OpenAI-compatible customer-support assistant with a refund tool and structured answer envelope.

entries.title: Sanitized ChatML role-boundary contract
entries.summary: A ChatML-style tokenizer_config is pinned by SHA-256 and verified for bounded role-boundary non-forgeability, demonstrating how escaping keeps user content from rendering as assistant control.
entries.real_world: ChatML-family local model served behind an OpenAI-compatible chat API.

entries.title: Training-to-serving static contract
entries.summary: A serving chat template and fine-tuning manifest are checked as finite static contracts so supervised targets remain aligned with the assistant role used at inference time.
entries.real_world: Instruction-tuning pipeline that must preserve serving-time role assumptions.

entries.title: Pinned RAG truncation budget
entries.summary: A retrieval payload and framework truncation policy are pinned and verified for citation survival, chunk-boundary stability, overlap accounting, metadata overhead, and payload limits.
entries.real_world: RAG question-answering service where citations and system policy must survive context-window pressure.

entries.title: Audited accepted-risk policy
entries.summary: A policy file suppresses an intentionally missing documentation fixture with owner, expiration, justification, and accepted-risk text, proving the gallery can explain risk rather than hiding it.
entries.real_world: Enterprise CI configuration that needs temporary, auditable exceptions without weakening unrelated checks.
